intent: Build the private PR admission gate, adversarial review trigger, contained validation workflow, and automatic merge arming before public launch.
claims: Privileged pull_request_target workflows load policy from the trusted base and do not execute candidate code.
Candidate validation runs with no network, a read-only repository mount, no Linux capabilities, and resource limits.
High-risk executable mutations fail closed until approved adversarial review evidence exists.
Adversarial review approvals count only when recorded against the exact current head commit.
Changed-file classification includes rename sources, so renames cannot move files out of protected-path matching.
Critical governance and red-zone changes never auto-merge and remain owner-only.
No files have been pushed to the public repository.
A seven-scenario local lab harness proves benign, high-risk, malicious, unauthorized red-zone, owner red-zone, rename-evasion, and missing-attestation behavior.
